key_insight: Physics-informed learning can be done with trainable B-splines instead of neural networks, giving a structured, smooth, and locally supported representation of the unknown PDE solution.
engineering_relevance: PI-Splines offer a compact and interpretable alternative for PDE-constrained modeling when geometric control, boundary handling, and parameter efficiency matter.
method: Represent the field with a tensor-product B-spline expansion and train the control coefficients.
Minimize PDE residuals in the same physics-informed framework used by PINNs.
Enforce compatible boundary conditions strongly by fixing boundary control coefficients.
evidence_signal: The method is tested on multiple benchmark PDE problems and compared against standard physics-informed baselines using matched equations, collocation points, loss terms, and optimization settings.
watch_item: The approach depends on the solution being compatible with a spline basis, so highly irregular geometries or features may require careful spline design.
paper_title: Trainable Spline Representations for Physics-Informed Learning
